title: FOIA Request – Body Cam & Dash Cam Footage – Kyle D. Ashworth – 2025-03-29

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

INCIDENT DETAILS
  Date/Time:  2025-03-29 at 03:10
  Location:   Pomeroy Avenue and Price Street, Pismo Beach, CA
  Suspect:    Kyle D. Ashworth
  Charges:    Evading a Peace Officer (VC 2800.1), Driving Under the Influence of Alcohol (VC 23152(a)), Reckless Driving (VC 23103)
  Case #:     PB25-00674

RECORDS REQUESTED

1. ALL body-worn camera (BWC) footage from each of the following officers and any additional officers who responded to or were present during this incident:

  • Officer J. Navarro Badge #PB-103 (Pursuing Officer)
  • Officer T. Garfield Badge #PB-114 (Assisting Officer)

2. ALL dash camera / in-car video footage from every patrol vehicle, marked or unmarked, involved in the pursuit or apprehension.

3. Any supplemental video including aerial/overhead, pole-mounted cameras, and surveillance footage retained as part of the investigation.

4. Audio dispatch recordings and radio communications from the initial call through the conclusion of the incident.

 Please provide records in original digital format (MP4 for video, WAV/MP3 for audio). Electronic delivery via secure download preferred.

I request a waiver of all fees. Disclosure concerns the conduct of public officials in performance of their duties and contributes to public understanding of government operations. This request is not primarily commercial.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brandon Martin
